##NAMES=Pseudobrookite
##RRUFFID=R140974
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=(Fe^3+^_2_Ti)O_5_
##LOCALITY=Emmelberg, Üdersdorf, Daun, Eifel, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Ch. and H. Schäfer
##DESCRIPTION=Dark brown tabular or elongated crystals. Associated with sanidine and mullite (supposedly).
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral has been confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140974
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 9.735 b: 9.917 c: 3.71 alpha: 90 beta: 90 gamma: 90 volume: 358.2 crystal system: orthorhombic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=780
